title: Force ETH transfers bypassing reverting receive() hooks
solidity_before:
(bool success, ) = to.call{value: amount}("");
require(success, "ETHTransferFailed"); // Fails if 'to' is a contract that reverts.
explanation: Attempts a standard ETH call with a defined gas stipend. If the target contract reverts (DoS attempt or disabled `receive` function), it falls back to deploying a temporary 22-byte contract using `create`. This mini-contract instantly executes `SELFDESTRUCT(to)`, forcefully pushing the ETH into the target address, bypassing all EVM fallback execution rules.
when_to_apply: Distributing native ETH to unknown addresses, performing protocol airdrops, or processing withdrawals where malicious receivers might try to DoS the transaction.
when_not_to_apply: On rollups or EVM networks where `SELFDESTRUCT` (EIP-6780) is heavily modified or completely disabled without SENDALL support.
